title: Song of Myself
author: Walt Whitman
content: “I celebrate myself, and sing myself... I am large, I contain multitudes.”
action: Acknowledge your own complexity and refuse to be defined by a single role.

title: The Road Not Taken
author: Robert Frost
content: “I took the one less traveled by, And that has made all the difference.”
action: Make the non-consensus choice on a current operational dilemma.

title: Moby-Dick
author: Herman Melville
content: “I know not all that may be coming, but be it what it will, I'll go to it laughing.”
action: Aggressively pursue the unknown objective without fearing the outcome.

title: The Great Gatsby
author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
content: “So we beat on, boats against the current, borne back ceaselessly into the past.”
action: Identify one 'past' habit pulling you backward and cut it.

title: Civil Disobedience
author: Henry David Thoreau
content: “If a man does not keep pace with his companions, perhaps it is because he hears a different drummer.”
action: Ignore a standard industry convention that provides no real value.
